classification_rationale: Repository contains only README.md and pyproject.toml with no source code, package, schemas, contracts, or tests. The afi_sdk package referenced in README usage examples does not exist. README Status line states "New repository created during multi-repo reorganization (2025-11-14)" and the single git commit is "chore: initialize new repository". It is an empty scaffold/placeholder, not yet a functioning SDK. Classified STALE (empty placeholder created during reorg, no implementation); could alternatively be SUPPORTING once built, but currently holds no protocol surface.
primary_purpose: Intended official Python SDK for building on AFI Protocol (signal submission/validation helpers, agent integration, ML/AI integration), but currently an empty scaffold with only README and pyproject.toml — no implementation exists.
protocol_touchpoints: USS/CPJ ingest (aspirational only: 'Signal submission and validation helpers')

contradictions.title: SDK targets afi-reactor as the API client surface
contradictions.detail: README lists 'Pythonic API clients for afi-reactor' as the headline feature, coupling the official SDK to the reference orchestrator/runtime (afi-reactor) rather than to the portable protocol dialects (USS v1.1 / CPJ v0.1) or the gateway ingest boundary. A portable-protocol SDK should target the canonical ingest dialect + schema validation, not a specific stack component. This is aspirational text only (no code), so impact is low for now.
contradictions.evidence: README.md:11 '- Pythonic API clients for afi-reactor'

contradictions.title: pyproject declares version 1.0.0 for an empty scaffold
contradictions.detail: pyproject.toml sets version = "1.0.0" while the repo has zero implementation, no afi_sdk package, and no tests. The README example `from afi_sdk import AFIClient` cannot work. Version signaling contradicts actual maturity.
contradictions.evidence: pyproject.toml:3 'version = "1.0.0"' vs README.md:41 '🚧 New repository created during multi-repo reorganization (2025-11-14)'

upstream: afi-reactor (named as API client target in README.md:11; not declared as a code dependency)

notable_findings.title: Empty scaffold repo — no implementation, schemas, or contracts present
notable_findings.evidence: git ls-files -> only 'README.md' and 'pyproject.toml'; find . -type f (excl .git) returns only those two; single commit '08c24dd chore: initialize new repository'
notable_findings.recommendation: Tag as not-yet-implemented placeholder; revisit classification (SUPPORTING) once SDK code lands. No protocol surface to audit currently.

notable_findings.title: Referenced afi_sdk Python package does not exist
notable_findings.evidence: README.md:28 'from afi_sdk import AFIClient' but no afi_sdk/ directory or .py files exist in repo
notable_findings.recommendation: Mark README usage as aspirational or scaffold the package; current README implies a working install that fails.

notable_findings.title: SDK feature set names afi-reactor instead of portable dialect/gateway
notable_findings.evidence: README.md:11 '- Pythonic API clients for afi-reactor'
notable_findings.recommendation: When built, target USS v1.1 / CPJ v0.1 ingest dialect + afi-config schema validation via the gateway boundary, keeping reactor as one optional backend, so the SDK stays portable across stacks.

notable_findings.title: No replay/determinism, vault/TSSD, mint/receipt, or schema artifacts
notable_findings.evidence: grep over README.md/pyproject.toml for mongodb|TSSD|vault|usignal|CPJ|mintForSignal|epoch|replay|determinism|BigQuery|postgresql|... returns only README copy 'afi-reactor', 'Signal submission', 'Submit a signal'
notable_findings.recommendation: None — repo holds no replay-critical artifacts and no determinism rules.
